references.cnsa_2_0: NSA CNSA 2.0 suite: ML-KEM, ML-DSA, AES-256, SHA-384/512

rules.message: RSA key generation / encryption is quantum-vulnerable (Shor's algorithm breaks the integer-factorization assumption).
rules.recommendation: For key establishment, migrate to ML-KEM-768 (FIPS 203), ideally in a hybrid X25519+ML-KEM-768 construction during transition.
rules.cnsa_note: CNSA 2.0 mandates ML-KEM for key establishment; RSA is not approved for NSS post-transition.

rules.message: ECDSA signatures are quantum-vulnerable (Shor's algorithm breaks the elliptic-curve discrete-log assumption).
rules.recommendation: Migrate signing to ML-DSA (FIPS 204); use SLH-DSA (FIPS 205) where a conservative hash-based backup is preferred.
rules.cnsa_note: CNSA 2.0 mandates ML-DSA for signatures; ECDSA is not approved for NSS post-transition.

rules.message: ECDH key exchange is quantum-vulnerable and exposed to Harvest-Now-Decrypt-Later: recorded sessions can be decrypted once a CRQC exists.
rules.recommendation: Adopt hybrid X25519+ML-KEM-768 key agreement (IANA group X25519MLKEM768, codepoint 0x11ec) during transition, then ML-KEM-768.
rules.cnsa_note: CNSA 2.0 mandates ML-KEM for key establishment.

rules.message: Finite-field Diffie-Hellman is quantum-vulnerable and exposed to Harvest-Now-Decrypt-Later.
rules.recommendation: Replace with ML-KEM-768 (FIPS 203) key establishment, hybridized with X25519 during transition.
rules.cnsa_note: CNSA 2.0 mandates ML-KEM for key establishment.

rules.message: DSA signatures rely on the discrete-log assumption and are quantum-vulnerable.
rules.recommendation: Migrate signing to ML-DSA (FIPS 204) or SLH-DSA (FIPS 205).

rules.message: MD5 is a broken hash (collisions) and is not quantum-resistant at any usable strength.
rules.recommendation: Use SHA-384 or SHA-512 (SHA-2 family) per CNSA 2.0; SHA-3 is also acceptable.
rules.cnsa_note: CNSA 2.0 mandates SHA-384 (or stronger) for hashing.

rules.message: SHA-1 is broken against collision attacks and below CNSA 2.0 strength.
rules.recommendation: Use SHA-384 or SHA-512 per CNSA 2.0.

rules.message: Triple-DES has a small effective key size and is deprecated; not CNSA 2.0 compliant.
rules.recommendation: Use AES-256-GCM per CNSA 2.0 (Grover's algorithm only halves symmetric strength, so AES-256 retains ~128-bit security).
rules.cnsa_note: CNSA 2.0 mandates AES-256 for symmetric encryption.

rules.message: Certificate artifact present; its signature algorithm and public key may be quantum-vulnerable (RSA/ECDSA).
rules.recommendation: Inventory certificate signature algorithms and key types; plan reissuance with ML-DSA once your PKI supports FIPS 204.
rules.cnsa_note: CNSA 2.0 mandates ML-DSA for signing, including code-signing and PKI, by the 2030-2033 window.
